title: Living in Boulder vlog 10 - 27 - 2017: Linking Google Earth BC battles to my Timeline

enrichment.summary: Kyle vlogs about his ongoing website project linking Google Earth locations to historical battles across centuries, discussing workflow challenges including Flickr being down. He also shares what he's been listening to — Aristotle's Politics and Polybius — and outlines a future project to reconstruct Hercules as a real historical figure based on legendary accounts.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: One of his favorites — unusually readable because it lists political systems and their causes like history, unlike Aristotle's typically dry scientific writing
The legends of Hercules should be reexamined as real history; the feats attributed to him were actual physical accomplishments, not myth
School treats figures like Hercules as mythology but Polybius and others treated them as history, and that framing deserves more attention
Progress is being made but complexity is growing — inserting missing battles and linking map locations is taking longer than anticipated

claims.subject: History
claims.claim: When you go to school and learn history class, you learn about Polimius or ancient Greece, which is all history.

claims.subject: History
claims.claim: We need to change and look at the history because they looked at history, even if we don't have it written the same way.

claims.subject: Hercules
claims.claim: Hercules' tour around the empire and all the layers that he did were actual physical things, like pulling a boat with an ox across the Strait of Sicily.

claims.subject: Hercules
claims.claim: Hercules was just a badass who really did those things.

claims.subject: History
claims.claim: There are lots of little stories like Hercules' that you can pick out to create history that we don't actually have.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Hopefully I'll do a video tomorrow.
